source: On The Tape / RiskReversal — Fear is the market killer
summary: Video game companies have strategic value exceeding financial value; consolidation in gaming will accelerate driven by subscription model dynamics

tickers_audited.reasoning: Baker explicitly named Microsoft as the acquirer in the Activision deal, which he uses as the central validation of his subscription-driven consolidation thesis. Microsoft's Xbox Game Pass is directly cited as a structural driver of the thesis.
Activision Blizzard is the direct subject of the Microsoft acquisition that Baker explicitly uses as the thesis-validating example of strategic value exceeding financial value in gaming.

Baker explicitly tied Roblox to the metaverse positioning and social network effects argument within this thesis — it is specifically named as an example of a dominant multiplayer platform with strategic value.

Sony/PlayStation is explicitly named by Baker as one of the two platform owners shifting to subscription models, making it a direct subject of the consolidation thesis rather than a tangential mention.

EA was not specifically named by Baker in connection with this thesis. It was added as an inferred beneficiary based on industry logic (independent publisher = acquisition target), not because Baker explicitly connected EA to this consolidation thesis.

Take-Two was not specifically named by Baker in connection with this thesis. Like EA, it was added as an inferred consolidation target based on general industry reasoning, not a direct Baker statement linking TTWO to this exact thesis.
